label: Kazan Khanate
description: The golden age of the Kazan Khanate — a successor state of the Golden Horde. The city was a major trading and cultural center connecting East and West.

label: Russian Empire
description: After Ivan the Terrible's conquest in 1552, Kazan became a key city of the Russian Empire. Architecture blended Orthodox churches with surviving Tatar mosques.

label: Soviet Era
description: Under Soviet rule, Kazan underwent industrialization. Many historic buildings were repurposed or demolished, while constructivist architecture appeared across the city.

label: Modern Kazan
description: Today's Kazan is a vibrant bilingual city — capital of Tatarstan. Renovated landmarks, the 2013 Universiade, and a booming cultural scene define the modern era.
